name: Cloudflare Cache Poisoning Header Normalization Pack
objective: Reduce cache poisoning and cache deception risk by normalizing and validating cache-influencing request headers and blocking ambiguous origin-routing/header-override patterns.
risk_mitigated: Host header poisoning against cache keys and upstream routing
Cache key confusion via X-Forwarded-Host/X-Host/X-Original-Host override headers
Cache deception and path confusion via X-Original-URL/X-Rewrite-URL
Split-view cache behavior caused by duplicate, malformed, or conflicting host/path signals
assumptions: Cloudflare is the internet-facing edge and primary cache layer for protected zones.
Applications either do not require client-supplied host override headers or can scope them to trusted upstream networks only.
Origin hostnames and expected public hostnames are documented before enforcement.

rollout: Phase 1 (Observe): Deploy rules in Log action only. Collect 7-14 days of traffic including peak and release windows.
Phase 2 (Friction): Move high-confidence anomalies to Managed Challenge/JS Challenge where user impact is acceptable.
Phase 3 (Enforce): Promote confirmed malicious/conflicting patterns to Block; keep environment-specific exceptions explicit and minimal.
Phase 4 (Steady State): Review exceptions monthly and remove stale allowances.
deployment_notes.rollback: If false positives spike, revert Block/Challenge rules to Log and retain matching expressions while adjusting scoped allow conditions (path, host, source ASN/IP, service token identity).

tuning_notes: If a trusted reverse proxy/load balancer in front of Cloudflare intentionally sets X-Forwarded-Host, allowlist only that source network or authenticated connector and normalize to a single canonical signal.
For multi-tenant SaaS, maintain an explicit allowlist/regex for valid hostnames and reject hostnames outside tenant provisioning data.
Exclude internal health-check paths and authenticated synthetic monitors if they use non-standard headers for diagnostics.
When legacy applications rely on X-Original-URL/X-Rewrite-URL, constrain accepted values to expected path prefixes and block absolute URL forms.
Avoid broad header-based bypasses; prefer narrow exceptions bound to specific hostname + path + trusted source.

signals: Count/rate of requests with conflicting Host vs X-Forwarded-Host values
Requests containing override headers (X-Original-URL, X-Rewrite-URL, X-Host, X-Forwarded-Host) by hostname and path
Block/challenge/log ratio per rule over time
Cache HIT/MISS/BYPASS shifts after enforcement changes
Origin 4xx/5xx deltas correlated with blocked header anomalies
Top client IP/ASN/User-Agent for matched events to separate scanners from legitimate integrations
false_positive_indicators: Sudden increase in matches from known corporate NATs, API partners, or uptime monitors
Elevated challenge solves on business-critical endpoints after rule promotion
Support tickets reporting intermittent redirects or wrong-content cache responses
Host/path-specific spikes immediately after deployment or app routing changes
monitoring.review_cadence: daily during first two weeks, then weekly

rules.name: Conflicting host authority indicators
rules.expression: (len(http.request.headers["x-forwarded-host"]) > 0 and lower(http.request.headers["x-forwarded-host"][0]) ne lower(http.host)) or (len(http.request.headers["x-host"]) > 0 and lower(http.request.headers["x-host"][0]) ne lower(http.host))
rules.description: Detects requests where host-override style headers conflict with the actual HTTP host used for routing/cache key decisions.

rules.name: Suspicious original/rewrite URL override headers
rules.expression: (len(http.request.headers["x-original-url"]) > 0 and (starts_with(lower(http.request.headers["x-original-url"][0]), "http://") or starts_with(lower(http.request.headers["x-original-url"][0]), "https://") or contains(http.request.headers["x-original-url"][0], ".."))) or (len(http.request.headers["x-rewrite-url"]) > 0 and (starts_with(lower(http.request.headers["x-rewrite-url"][0]), "http://") or starts_with(lower(http.request.headers["x-rewrite-url"][0]), "https://") or contains(http.request.headers["x-rewrite-url"][0], "..")))
rules.description: Flags URL override headers carrying absolute URLs or traversal-like sequences often used in cache deception/poisoning attempts.

rules.name: Multiple host override headers present
rules.expression: (len(http.request.headers["x-forwarded-host"]) > 0 and len(http.request.headers["x-original-host"]) > 0) or (len(http.request.headers["x-forwarded-host"]) > 0 and len(http.request.headers["x-host"]) > 0) or (len(http.request.headers["x-original-host"]) > 0 and len(http.request.headers["x-host"]) > 0)
rules.description: Detects stacked host-override signaling that increases ambiguity and cache key confusion risk.

normalization_recommendations: Use Cloudflare Transform Rules to remove untrusted host/path override headers at edge when not required by application design.
Canonicalize host handling to a single trusted header/source; do not permit client-controlled alternates to affect origin selection.
Ensure cache key policy does not include attacker-controlled override headers unless strictly validated and normalized.
Enforce hostname allowlists for public zones and redirect/block unknown hostnames before cache lookup where possible.
